mood.explanation: It's completely normal to feel this way. Your body is just preparing you for a challenge by giving you extra energy.

actions.action: Take 3 deep breaths (inhale for 4s, hold for 4s, exhale for 4s).
Focus on one friendly face in the room.
Remind yourself: "I have prepared for this."

mood.quote: Confidence comes not from always being right, but from not fearing to be wrong.

mood.explanation: Being shy means you are observant and thoughtful. You don't have to be the loudest person to make an impact.

actions.action: Start a conversation with just 1 person today.
Smile and make eye contact when someone speaks to you.
Ask one simple question to show interest.

mood.quote: Your voice matters, even if it shakes.

mood.explanation: You are in a great state of mind! Use this energy to push your boundaries further.

actions.action: Volunteer for a task or answer a question.
Compliment someone else to share your positive energy.
Take the lead on a small project.

mood.quote: Believe you can and you're halfway there.

challenges.challenge: Ask a question in class or a meeting.
Give a genuine compliment to a stranger.
Maintain eye contact during a conversation for at least 5 seconds.
Initiate a small talk conversation (e.g., about the weather or weekend).
Speak up clearly when ordering food or coffee.

scenario.title: Job Interview
scenario.sample: Hi, I'm [Name]. I have a background in [Field] and I am passionate about solving complex problems. I'm excited about this opportunity to contribute to your team.

scenario.title: Talking to a Stranger
scenario.prompt: Start a casual conversation with someone next to you.
scenario.sample: Hi there! I couldn't help but notice your book. Is it a good read?

scenario.title: Presentation
scenario.prompt: Open a presentation about a topic you love.
scenario.sample: Good morning everyone. Today, I'm thrilled to share with you why [Topic] is not just interesting, but absolutely essential for our future.

overthinking.advice: Pause. Look around and name 5 things you can see, 4 things you can touch, 3 things you can hear, 2 things you can smell, and 1 thing you can taste.
Is this going to matter in 5 years? If not, don't spend more than 5 minutes worrying about it.
You are not your thoughts. Let them pass like clouds in the sky.

quickReplies.reply: That's a really interesting point. Tell me more!
I'm not sure, but let me think about it and get back to you.
